WA.rightToRepairCitation: RCW § 64.50.001 et seq. (Construction Defect Claims Act - Notice and Opportunity to Repair)

WV.antiIndemnityNote: W. Va. Code § 55-8-14 limits indemnification provisions in construction contracts that would indemnify for the indemnitee's own negligence
WV.impliedWarrantyNote: West Virginia recognizes implied warranty of habitability for new residential construction; Sewell v. Gregory

WI.antiIndemnityNote: Wis. Stat. § 895.447 prohibits indemnification provisions in construction contracts that would require a party to indemnify for the other party's negligence
WI.impliedWarrantyNote: Wisconsin recognizes implied warranty of habitability for new residential construction; Onalaska Electric Heating, Inc. v. Schaller
WI.notes: Wisconsin has a detailed lien law and prompt payment requirements under Wis. Stat. § 779.

WY.antiIndemnityNote: Wyo. Stat. § 30-1-131 prohibits indemnification provisions in oil and gas and construction contracts that would indemnify for the indemnitee's own negligence
WY.impliedWarrantyNote: Wyoming recognizes implied warranty of habitability for new residential construction; Tavares v. Horseshoe Lake Homeowners Ass'n
WY.notes: Wyoming anti-indemnity statute applies to both construction and oil and gas contracts. Wyoming has relatively few construction-specific statutes compared to other states.

DC.antiIndemnityNote: D.C. Code § 27A-202 limits unenforceable indemnity language that attempts to shift liability for a party's own negligence in construction contexts.
DC.impliedWarrantyNote: District courts recognize implied warranties in construction and residential habitability contexts based on common-law contract principles.
DC.notes: District procurement and construction contracts often layer federal and local requirements; verify agency-specific clauses on public work.

AL.citation: Ala. Admin. Code r. 335-6-12; ADEM Admin. Code r. 335-6-6
AL.erosionControlStandard: Alabama Handbook for Erosion Control, Sediment Control and Stormwater Management on Construction Sites
AL.swpppNote: SWPPP required for land disturbance ≥1 acre under NPDES Construction General Permit
AL.wetlandSetbackNote: Regulated by Army Corps Section 404 and Section 10; no additional state setback beyond federal

AK.notes: Alaska administers its own APDES program; state wetland program administered through ADEC.
AK.npdesAuthority: Alaska Department of Environmental Conservation (ADEC) - APDES Program
AK.swpppNote: SWPPP required under Alaska Pollutant Discharge Elimination System (APDES) for land disturbance ≥1 acre
AK.wetlandSetbackNote: Army Corps Section 404 applies; ADEC administers state water quality certification (401)

AZ.erosionControlStandard: Arizona Pollutant Discharge Elimination System (AZPDES) Construction General Permit
AZ.notes: Arizona not delegated for NPDES; EPA issues federal permits. ADEQ administers AZPDES under state law for some programs.
AZ.npdesAuthority: U.S. Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) Region 9 (Arizona not delegated)
AZ.swpppNote: SWPPP required under EPA Construction General Permit for land disturbance ≥1 acre; ADEQ administers AZPDES for some sectors
AZ.wetlandSetbackNote: Army Corps Section 404; ADWR and ADEQ administer state water quality certifications

AR.citation: Ark. Code Ann. § 8-4-201 et seq.; Reg. 6
AR.erosionControlStandard: Arkansas Storm Water Pollution Prevention Plan Requirements
AR.npdesAuthority: Arkansas Department of Energy and Environment (ADEE) / Arkansas Division of Environmental Quality (DEQ)
AR.swpppNote: SWPPP required under NPDES Construction General Permit for ≥1 acre disturbance
AR.wetlandSetbackNote: Army Corps Section 404; ADEE administers Section 401 water quality certification

CA.citation: Cal. Water Code § 13260 et seq.; Cal. Fish & Game Code § 1600 et seq.
CA.erosionControlStandard: California Stormwater Quality Association (CASQA) Construction BMP Handbook
CA.notes: California has one of the most extensive state environmental programs. CEQA review required for most projects. Coastal Development Permit required in coastal zone.
CA.npdesAuthority: California State Water Resources Control Board (SWRCB) / Regional Water Quality Control Boards
CA.swpppNote: SWPPP required under California Construction General Permit (Order 2009-0009-DWQ); Risk Level 1/2/3 determination required
CA.wetlandSetbackNote: State wetlands regulated by CDFW (Streambed Alteration Agreements), Regional Water Boards, and Coastal Commission; additional 100-ft buffer in some regions

CO.erosionControlStandard: Colorado Stormwater Management Plan requirements under CDPS permit
CO.notes: Colorado has additional air quality permits required during construction in ozone non-attainment areas.
CO.npdesAuthority: Colorado Department of Public Health and Environment (CDPHE) - Water Quality Control Division
CO.stateEnvAgency: Colorado Department of Public Health and Environment (CDPHE)
CO.swpppNote: SWPPP required under Colorado Discharge Permit System (CDPS) Construction General Permit for ≥1 acre disturbance
CO.wetlandSetbackNote: Army Corps Section 404; CDPHE administers Section 401 certification

CT.citation: CGS § 22a-36 et seq. (Inland Wetlands Act); CGS § 22a-361 (Structures in Tidal Wetlands)
CT.erosionControlStandard: Connecticut Guidelines for Soil Erosion and Sediment Control
CT.notes: Connecticut has a robust state wetlands program separate from federal. Local Inland Wetlands Commissions also regulate development near wetlands.
CT.npdesAuthority: Connecticut Department of Energy and Environmental Protection (DEEP)
CT.stateEnvAgency: Connecticut Department of Energy and Environmental Protection (DEEP)
CT.swpppNote: SWPPP required under General Permit for the Discharge of Stormwater and Dewatering Wastewaters from Construction Activities
CT.wetlandSetbackNote: State wetlands and watercourses regulated by DEEP and local inland wetlands commissions; 50-100 ft setbacks typical in local regulations

DE.citation: 7 Del. Code § 6001 et seq. (Sediment and Stormwater); 7 Del. Code § 6601 et seq. (Wetlands)
DE.erosionControlStandard: Delaware Erosion and Sediment Control Handbook
DE.notes: Delaware requires a Sediment and Stormwater Management Plan approved before land disturbance. Wetland jurisdictional determination required.
DE.npdesAuthority: Delaware Department of Natural Resources and Environmental Control (DNREC)
DE.stateEnvAgency: Delaware Department of Natural Resources and Environmental Control (DNREC)
DE.swpppNote: SWPPP required for land disturbance ≥1 acre under DNREC Sediment and Stormwater Program
DE.wetlandSetbackNote: State Wetlands Act regulates tidal wetlands; 50-ft buffer required for many activities near wetlands

DC.citation: District stormwater and erosion/sediment control regulations under DOEE authority
DC.erosionControlStandard: District erosion and sediment control requirements with DOEE stormwater management criteria
DC.notes: District projects typically require coordinated ESC/SWPPP and stormwater retention compliance through DOEE permitting workflows.
DC.npdesAuthority: District Department of Energy and Environment (DOEE)
DC.stateEnvAgency: District Department of Energy and Environment (DOEE)
DC.swpppNote: SWPPP and erosion/sediment controls are required for qualifying land disturbance under District stormwater rules
DC.wetlandSetbackNote: Wetland and water-resource impacts are reviewed by DOEE with federal Section 404/401 coordination when applicable

FL.citation: Fla. Stat. § 373.413; Fla. Admin. Code r. 62-330
FL.erosionControlStandard: Florida Stormwater, Erosion and Sedimentation Control Inspector's Manual
FL.notes: Florida regulates wetlands through Environmental Resource Permit (ERP) system administered by FDEP and five Water Management Districts. No net loss of wetlands policy.
FL.swpppNote: SWPPP required under FDEP Generic Permit for Stormwater Discharge from Large and Small Construction Activities
FL.wetlandSetbackNote: Environmental Resource Permit (ERP) system regulates wetland impacts; 25-ft minimum upland buffer typical; varies by Water Management District

GA.citation: O.C.G.A. § 12-7-1 et seq. (ESCP Act); § 12-5-280 et seq. (Marshlands)
GA.erosionControlStandard: Manual for Erosion and Sediment Control in Georgia (Green Book)
GA.notes: Georgia requires a Land Disturbance Permit (LDP) for most grading activities. Issued by local issuing authorities under state oversight.
GA.stateEnvAgency: Georgia Department of Natural Resources - Environmental Protection Division
GA.swpppNote: SWPPP required under NPDES General Permit for Stormwater Runoff from Land Disturbing Activities (GAR100002)
GA.wetlandSetbackNote: Army Corps Section 404; Georgia DNR and EPD administer state water quality certification; coastal marshlands regulated by GADNR

HI.erosionControlStandard: Hawaii Stormwater Best Management Practices Manual
HI.notes: Hawaii has strict coastal zone management requirements under the Hawaii CZM Program.
HI.npdesAuthority: Hawaii Department of Health (DOH) - Clean Water Branch
HI.swpppNote: SWPPP required under NPDES General Permit for Storm Water Discharges from Construction Activities (HIG100000)
HI.wetlandSetbackNote: Army Corps Section 404; Hawaii DOH Section 401 certification; Coastal Zone Management program applies in coastal areas

ID.erosionControlStandard: EPA Construction General Permit BMP requirements; IDEQ stormwater guidance
ID.notes: Idaho not delegated for NPDES construction stormwater; EPA issues federal CGP. IDEQ administers Section 401 certifications.
ID.npdesAuthority: U.S. Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) Region 10 (Idaho not delegated for construction stormwater)
ID.swpppNote: SWPPP required under EPA Construction General Permit (CGP); IDEQ also issues land application permits
ID.wetlandSetbackNote: Army Corps Section 404; IDEQ administers Section 401 water quality certification

IL.erosionControlStandard: Illinois Urban Manual - NRCS approved conservation practices
IL.notes: Illinois requires a Notice of Intent (NOI) before starting land disturbing activities. IEPA issues NPDES permits.
IL.swpppNote: SWPPP required under NPDES General NPDES Permit for Stormwater Discharges from Construction Site Activities (ILR10)
IL.wetlandSetbackNote: Army Corps Section 404; IEPA Section 401 certification; Chicago has additional local wetland ordinances

IN.notes: Indiana regulates isolated wetlands not covered by federal jurisdiction through state law.
IN.swpppNote: SWPPP required under Indiana General Permit Rule 5 (327 IAC 15-5) for land disturbing activities ≥1 acre
IN.wetlandSetbackNote: Army Corps Section 404; IDEM administers Section 401 certification; isolated wetlands regulated by IDEM

IA.swpppNote: SWPPP required under NPDES General Permit for Stormwater Discharge Associated with Industrial Activity for Construction Activities (NPDES No. 2)
IA.wetlandSetbackNote: Army Corps Section 404; Iowa DNR Section 401 certification

KS.npdesAuthority: Kansas Department of Health and Environment (KDHE)
KS.stateEnvAgency: Kansas Department of Health and Environment (KDHE)
KS.swpppNote: SWPPP required under NPDES General Permit for Stormwater Runoff from Construction Activities (KSCGP)
KS.wetlandSetbackNote: Army Corps Section 404; KDHE Section 401 certification

KY.erosionControlStandard: Kentucky Erosion Prevention and Sediment Control Field Guide
KY.npdesAuthority: Kentucky Energy and Environment Cabinet - Division of Water
KY.swpppNote: SWPPP required under Kentucky NPDES General Permit for Stormwater Discharges Associated with Construction Activities (KYG010000)
KY.wetlandSetbackNote: Army Corps Section 404; KY Division of Water Section 401 certification; isolated wetlands regulated under state law

LA.citation: La. Rev. Stat. § 30:2073 et seq.; La. Rev. Stat. § 49:214.21 et seq. (Coastal Zone)
LA.erosionControlStandard: Louisiana Stormwater Construction General Permit BMP requirements
LA.notes: Louisiana Coastal Use Permit required for most construction activity in the coastal zone. Extensive wetland mitigation requirements.
LA.swpppNote: SWPPP required under Louisiana Pollutant Discharge Elimination System (LPDES) General Permit LAG470000
LA.wetlandSetbackNote: Army Corps Section 404 and Section 10 for coastal waters; USACE New Orleans District jurisdiction extensive; LDNR Coastal Use Permits required in Coastal Zone

ME.citation: 38 M.R.S.A. § 480-A et seq. (NRPA); § 481 et seq. (Site Location)
ME.erosionControlStandard: Maine Erosion and Sediment Control BMPs
ME.notes: Maine NRPA is one of the most protective state wetland programs. 75-ft buffer required; permit needed for most activities in protected natural resources zones.
ME.swpppNote: SWPPP required under Maine NPDES Multi-Sector General Permit; also required for projects with land disturbance ≥20,000 sq ft under Site Location of Development Act
ME.wetlandSetbackNote: Natural Resources Protection Act (NRPA) requires permit for activities within 75 ft of wetlands, great ponds, rivers, and streams

MD.citation: Md. Code Ann., Env. § 4-101 et seq. (Nontidal Wetlands); Art. 66B (Chesapeake Bay Critical Area)
MD.erosionControlStandard: Maryland Standards and Specifications for Soil Erosion and Sediment Control
MD.notes: Maryland has extensive Chesapeake Bay Critical Area regulations and a robust state nontidal wetlands program.
MD.swpppNote: Sediment Control Plan required for disturbances ≥5,000 sq ft (0.17 acres) under Grading and Sediment Control law; NPDES CGP for ≥1 acre
MD.wetlandSetbackNote: 100-Year Critical Areas 1,000-ft buffer from Chesapeake Bay; state nontidal wetlands permit required; 25-ft minimum non-disturbance buffer

MA.notes: Massachusetts not delegated NPDES; EPA issues CGP. Massachusetts Wetlands Protection Act is one of the strongest state wetland protection laws. Local Conservation Commissions enforce WPA.
MA.npdesAuthority: U.S. Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) Region 1 (Massachusetts not delegated for NPDES)
MA.swpppNote: SWPPP required under EPA CGP; MassDEP Wetlands Protection Act permits required; land disturbance ≥1 acre requires NOI
MA.wetlandSetbackNote: Wetlands Protection Act requires 100-ft buffer zone around wetlands; Order of Conditions required for work within buffer zone

MI.citation: MCL § 324.30301 et seq. (Part 303 Wetlands); MCL § 324.8701 (Part 31 NPDES)
MI.notes: Michigan regulates all wetlands under Part 303 of the Natural Resources and Environmental Protection Act (NREPA), including isolated wetlands.
MI.npdesAuthority: Michigan Department of Environment, Great Lakes, and Energy (EGLE)
MI.stateEnvAgency: Michigan Department of Environment, Great Lakes, and Energy (EGLE)
MI.swpppNote: SWPPP required under Michigan NPDES Phase II Stormwater General Permit (MIG010000) for ≥1 acre land disturbance
MI.wetlandSetbackNote: Michigan Wetlands Protection Act regulates all wetlands including isolated; state permit required for most impacts; no fixed setback but buffer often required

MN.notes: Minnesota WCA provides robust protections for all wetlands. Avoidance, minimization, and mitigation sequencing required.
MN.swpppNote: SWPPP required under MPCA Construction Stormwater General Permit (MN R100001) for land disturbance ≥1 acre
MN.wetlandSetbackNote: Minnesota Wetlands Conservation Act (WCA) regulates wetlands; no-net-loss policy; sequencing required; local WCA authority administers

MS.erosionControlStandard: Mississippi Erosion and Sediment Control Specifications
MS.notes: Coastal wetlands regulated by Mississippi Department of Marine Resources in coastal counties.
MS.swpppNote: SWPPP required under MSDES General Permit MSR10 for construction activities disturbing ≥1 acre
MS.wetlandSetbackNote: Army Corps Section 404; MDEQ Section 401 water quality certification; coastal wetlands regulated by MDMR

MO.citation: Mo. Rev. Stat. § 644.016 et seq.; 10 CSR 20-6.200
MO.erosionControlStandard: Missouri Land Disturbance General Permit BMP requirements
MO.npdesAuthority: Missouri Department of Natural Resources (MDNR) - Land Reclamation Program
MO.swpppNote: SWPPP (SWPPP/SWP3) required under Missouri NPDES General Permit for Land Disturbance (MOR10) for ≥1 acre
MO.wetlandSetbackNote: Army Corps Section 404; MDNR Section 401 water quality certification

MT.citation: Mont. Code Ann. § 75-5-401 et seq.; MCA § 75-7-101 (310 Permit)
MT.erosionControlStandard: Montana Storm Water Management Program for Construction Activities
MT.notes: Montana 310 Permit required from local conservation district for activities in or near perennial streams and rivers.
MT.swpppNote: SWPPP required under Montana Pollutant Discharge Elimination System (MPDES) General Permit MTR100000 for ≥1 acre
MT.wetlandSetbackNote: Army Corps Section 404; Montana DEQ Section 401 certification; 310 Permit required for work in/near streams and rivers

NE.npdesAuthority: Nebraska Department of Environment and Energy (NDEE)
NE.stateEnvAgency: Nebraska Department of Environment and Energy (NDEE)
NE.swpppNote: SWPPP required under NPDES General Permit for Stormwater Discharges from Construction Activities (NER010000) for ≥1 acre
NE.wetlandSetbackNote: Army Corps Section 404; NDEE Section 401 certification

NV.erosionControlStandard: Nevada Stormwater Best Management Practices Handbook
NV.swpppNote: SWPPP required under Nevada Pollutant Discharge Elimination System (NPDES) General Permit for Construction Activities (NVR100000) for ≥1 acre
NV.wetlandSetbackNote: Army Corps Section 404; NDEP Section 401 certification; extensive arid land regulations

NH.citation: RSA 482-A (Fill and Dredge in Wetlands); RSA 483-B (Shoreland Protection)
NH.notes: New Hampshire not delegated NPDES. DES Wetlands Bureau regulates all wetlands under RSA 482-A. Alteration of Terrain (AOT) permit required.
NH.npdesAuthority: U.S. Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) Region 1 (New Hampshire not delegated for NPDES)
NH.stateEnvAgency: New Hampshire Department of Environmental Services (DES)
NH.swpppNote: SWPPP required under EPA CGP; NH DES Alteration of Terrain permit required for disturbances ≥100,000 sq ft
NH.wetlandSetbackNote: Wetlands Bureau regulates all wetlands; 50-ft setback (Shoreland Water Quality Protection Act) near surface waters; Prime Wetlands designation for highest quality wetlands

NJ.citation: N.J.S.A. 13:9B-1 et seq. (FWPA); N.J.S.A. 13:19-1 et seq. (CAFRA)
NJ.erosionControlStandard: NJ Standards for Soil Erosion and Sediment Control
NJ.notes: New Jersey has one of the most protective state wetland programs. NJFWPA transition area requirements often more restrictive than federal Section 404.
NJ.npdesAuthority: New Jersey Department of Environmental Protection (NJDEP)
NJ.stateEnvAgency: New Jersey Department of Environmental Protection (NJDEP)
NJ.swpppNote: SWPPP required under New Jersey Pollutant Discharge Elimination System (NJPDES) Stormwater General Permit 2 (NJG0088323) for ≥1 acre
NJ.wetlandSetbackNote: State Freshwater Wetlands Protection Act requires 50-ft transition areas around most wetlands; 150-ft for exceptional resource value; Coastal Area Facility Review Act (CAFRA) in coastal zone

NM.erosionControlStandard: EPA CGP BMP requirements; NMED Stormwater Program guidance
NM.notes: New Mexico not delegated NPDES; EPA issues permits. Acequia systems (irrigation ditches) have special legal protections. Groundwater protection important in arid regions.
NM.npdesAuthority: U.S. Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) Region 6 (New Mexico not delegated for NPDES)
NM.swpppNote: SWPPP required under EPA Construction General Permit; NMED regulates under state Stormwater Program for some activities
NM.wetlandSetbackNote: Army Corps Section 404; NMED Section 401 certification; acequia systems and ditch associations have additional water rights protections

NY.citation: ECL Art. 24 (Freshwater Wetlands); ECL Art. 25 (Tidal Wetlands); 6 NYCRR Part 608
NY.erosionControlStandard: New York Standards and Specifications for Erosion and Sediment Control (Blue Book)
NY.notes: New York regulates freshwater wetlands ≥12.4 acres and all adjacent areas within 100 feet. NYC has additional wetland and watercourse regulations under Local Law.
NY.npdesAuthority: New York State Department of Environmental Conservation (NYSDEC)
NY.stateEnvAgency: New York State Department of Environmental Conservation (NYSDEC)
NY.swpppNote: SWPPP required under SPDES General Permit for Stormwater Discharges from Construction Activity (GP-0-20-001) for ≥1 acre land disturbance
NY.wetlandSetbackNote: Article 24 (Freshwater Wetlands) requires DEC permit for activities within 100-ft adjacent area; Article 25 (Tidal Wetlands) for coastal areas; extensive local wetland regulations in NYC

NC.citation: N.C. Gen. Stat. § 113A-50 et seq. (Sedimentation Pollution Control Act); 15A NCAC 04B
NC.erosionControlStandard: North Carolina Erosion and Sediment Control Planning and Design Manual
NC.notes: NC Sedimentation Pollution Control Act requires ESC Plan approval before land-disturbing activity on ≥1 acre. Riparian buffer rules are among the most protective in the Southeast.
NC.npdesAuthority: North Carolina Department of Environmental Quality (NCDEQ) - Division of Energy, Mineral and Land Resources
NC.stateEnvAgency: North Carolina Department of Environmental Quality (NCDEQ)
NC.swpppNote: Erosion and Sediment Control Plan required for disturbances ≥1 acre; NPDES Construction General Permit (NCG010000) required
NC.wetlandSetbackNote: Riparian buffers required: 50-ft for nutrient sensitive waters; 30-ft for other surface waters under Neuse and Tar-Pamlico Buffer Rules; DWR Section 401 certification

ND.citation: N.D. Cent. Code § 61-28-01 et seq.; NDAC 33.1-16-03
ND.npdesAuthority: North Dakota Department of Environmental Quality (NDDEQ)
ND.stateEnvAgency: North Dakota Department of Environmental Quality (NDDEQ)
ND.swpppNote: SWPPP required under NDPDES Construction General Permit (NDR10-0000) for ≥1 acre land disturbance
ND.wetlandSetbackNote: Army Corps Section 404; NDDEQ Section 401 certification; North Dakota Wetland Conservation Act provides some state protections

OH.citation: ORC § 6111.01 et seq. (Water Pollution Control); ORC § 6111.022 (Isolated Wetlands)
OH.erosionControlStandard: Ohio Rainwater and Land Development Manual
OH.notes: Ohio regulates isolated wetlands under state Isolated Wetland Permit program. Ohio EPA Section 401 certification required for all federal permits.
OH.swpppNote: SWPPP required under Ohio NPDES Construction General Permit (OHC000004) for ≥1 acre land disturbance
OH.wetlandSetbackNote: Army Corps Section 404; Ohio EPA Section 401 certification; isolated wetlands regulated under state Isolated Wetland Permit program

OK.erosionControlStandard: Oklahoma Stormwater Quality Management Program for Construction Activities
OK.notes: Oklahoma partial NPDES delegation; some permits administered by EPA. ODEQ administers state water quality program.
OK.npdesAuthority: Oklahoma Department of Environmental Quality (ODEQ) - partial delegation
OK.swpppNote: SWPPP required under ODEQ/EPA Construction General Permit for ≥1 acre land disturbance
OK.wetlandSetbackNote: Army Corps Section 404; ODEQ Section 401 certification

OR.erosionControlStandard: Oregon Erosion Prevention and Sediment Control Planning and Design Manual
OR.notes: Oregon DSL Removal-Fill Law regulates all fill/removal in waters of the state including wetlands. DSL individual permits required for significant impacts. Land Use Planning (Goal 5) provides additional protection.
OR.stateEnvAgency: Oregon Department of Environmental Quality (DEQ) / Oregon Department of State Lands (DSL)
OR.swpppNote: SWPPP required under Oregon 1200-C Construction General Permit for ≥1 acre land disturbance
OR.wetlandSetbackNote: DSL Removal-Fill Law regulates activities in wetlands; Statewide Planning Goal 5 protects significant wetlands; 50-ft minimum riparian setback typical in local regulations

PA.erosionControlStandard: Pennsylvania Erosion and Sediment Pollution Control Program Manual
PA.notes: Pennsylvania requires PNDI (Pennsylvania Natural Diversity Inventory) screening before construction. Chapter 105 permits cover activities in waters including wetlands.
PA.swpppNote: E&S Plan and PNDI review required for disturbances ≥5,000 sq ft; PADEP NPDES General Permit (PAG-02) for ≥1 acre
PA.wetlandSetbackNote: Chapter 105 (Water Obstruction and Encroachment) permit required for activities in/near wetlands and waterways; general permit GP-11 for minor impacts; 50-ft riparian buffer common

RI.citation: R.I. Gen. Laws § 2-1-18 et seq. (Freshwater Wetlands); § 46-23-1 et seq. (CRMC)
RI.erosionControlStandard: Rhode Island Stormwater Design and Installation Standards Manual
RI.notes: Rhode Island requires RIDEM assent for most activities near wetlands. Coastal wetlands regulated by CRMC (Coastal Resources Management Council).
RI.npdesAuthority: Rhode Island Department of Environmental Management (RIDEM)
RI.stateEnvAgency: Rhode Island Department of Environmental Management (RIDEM)
RI.swpppNote: SWPPP required under Rhode Island RIPDES Construction General Permit (RIR10I000) for ≥1 acre land disturbance
RI.wetlandSetbackNote: Freshwater Wetlands Act requires assent for activities within 50 ft of freshwater wetlands; 200-ft buffer around vernal pools; coastal wetlands regulated by Coastal Resources Management Council (CRMC)

SC.citation: S.C. Code Ann. § 48-14-10 et seq. (Stormwater); § 48-43-10 et seq. (Beachfront)
SC.erosionControlStandard: South Carolina Stormwater Management BMP Manual
SC.notes: Coastal Critical Area Permit required for development in the critical area within 2,500 ft of the Atlantic Ocean shoreline.
SC.npdesAuthority: South Carolina Department of Environmental Services (SCDES, formerly SCDHEC)
SC.stateEnvAgency: South Carolina Department of Environmental Services (SCDES)
SC.swpppNote: SWPPP required under NPDES Construction General Permit (SCR100000) for ≥1 acre land disturbance
SC.wetlandSetbackNote: Army Corps Section 404; SCDES Section 401 water quality certification; coastal critical area permit required in Beachfront Management Act zone

SD.erosionControlStandard: South Dakota Construction Site Stormwater BMPs
SD.npdesAuthority: South Dakota Department of Agriculture and Natural Resources (DANR)
SD.stateEnvAgency: South Dakota Department of Agriculture and Natural Resources (DANR)
SD.swpppNote: SWPPP required under South Dakota NPDES Construction General Permit (SDR100000) for ≥1 acre disturbance
SD.wetlandSetbackNote: Army Corps Section 404; DANR Section 401 certification; state has significant Prairie Pothole wetland areas

TN.citation: TCA § 69-3-101 et seq. (Water Quality Control Act); ARAP Rule 1200-04-07
TN.erosionControlStandard: Tennessee Erosion and Sediment Control Manual
TN.notes: Tennessee Aquatic Resource Alteration Permit (ARAP) required for stream/wetland impacts. Coordinated with USACE Section 404.
TN.npdesAuthority: Tennessee Department of Environment and Conservation (TDEC)
TN.stateEnvAgency: Tennessee Department of Environment and Conservation (TDEC)
TN.swpppNote: SWPPP required under NPDES Multi-Sector General Permit (TNR100000) for ≥1 acre land disturbance; also requires Construction Stormwater Permit
TN.wetlandSetbackNote: Army Corps Section 404; TDEC Section 401 water quality certification; Aquatic Resource Alteration Permit (ARAP) required for alterations to streams and wetlands

TX.citation: Tex. Water Code § 26.001 et seq.; 30 TAC § 281
TX.erosionControlStandard: Texas Commission on Environmental Quality Stormwater BMP Program
TX.notes: Texas has its own TPDES program. Large land development in coastal areas requires additional GLO permits. Edwards Aquifer recharge zone has special construction requirements.
TX.npdesAuthority: Texas Commission on Environmental Quality (TCEQ) - Texas Pollutant Discharge Elimination System (TPDES)
TX.swpppNote: SWPPP required under TPDES Construction General Permit (TXR150000) for ≥1 acre land disturbance; Stormwater Pollution Prevention Plan required
TX.wetlandSetbackNote: Army Corps Section 404 (Galveston District); TCEQ Section 401 certification; coastal areas regulated by Texas General Land Office

UT.npdesAuthority: Utah Division of Water Quality (DWQ) - Utah Pollutant Discharge Elimination System (UPDES)
UT.swpppNote: SWPPP required under UPDES Construction General Permit (UTR300000) for ≥1 acre land disturbance
UT.wetlandSetbackNote: Army Corps Section 404; Utah DWQ Section 401 certification; Great Salt Lake wetlands have special regulatory protections

VT.erosionControlStandard: Vermont Low Risk Site Handbook for Erosion Prevention and Sediment Control
VT.notes: Vermont Act 250 is a significant statewide land use permit required for major developments. Vermont Wetlands Rules administered by DEC Wetlands Program.
VT.swpppNote: SWPPP required under Vermont Construction General Permit (CGP) for ≥1 acre land disturbance; Vermont also requires Act 250 Land Use Permit for development >1 acre or 10 dwelling units
VT.wetlandSetbackNote: Vermont Wetlands Rules protect all wetlands; 50-ft buffer to significant wetlands; Act 250 review for larger projects near wetlands

VA.citation: Code of Va. § 62.1-44.15 et seq. (VESMP); § 28.2-1300 et seq. (VMRC Wetlands)
VA.erosionControlStandard: Virginia Erosion and Stormwater Management Program (VESMP) Regulations
VA.notes: Chesapeake Bay Preservation Act requires 100-ft Resource Protection Area (RPA) buffer along tidal shorelines and other water features. VMRC regulates tidal wetlands.
VA.swpppNote: SWPPP required under Virginia Pollution Abatement (VPA) / VPDES Construction General Permit (VAG11) for ≥1 acre land disturbance
VA.wetlandSetbackNote: Army Corps Section 404; VDEQ Section 401 certification; Virginia Marine Resources Commission (VMRC) for tidal wetlands; Chesapeake Bay Act RPA 100-ft buffer

WA.citation: RCW § 90.48 (Water Pollution Control); RCW § 90.58 (Shoreline Management Act); WAC 173-26
WA.erosionControlStandard: Washington State Department of Ecology Stormwater Management Manual for Western Washington (SWMMWW) and Eastern Washington (SWMMEW)
WA.notes: Washington has comprehensive state wetland regulations. SMA Substantial Development Permits required for projects within 200 ft of shorelines. Hydraulic Project Approval (HPA) required from WDFW for work near fish-bearing waters.
WA.swpppNote: SWPPP required under Construction Stormwater General Permit (WAR100000) for ≥1 acre land disturbance; turbidity monitoring required at many sites
WA.wetlandSetbackNote: Critical Areas Ordinances (CAOs) required by all counties and cities; typical 50-200 ft wetland buffer depending on wetland category and local code; DOE also regulates under Shoreline Management Act

WV.erosionControlStandard: West Virginia Stormwater Management and Design Guidance Manual
WV.npdesAuthority: West Virginia Department of Environmental Protection (WVDEP)
WV.stateEnvAgency: West Virginia Department of Environmental Protection (WVDEP)
WV.swpppNote: SWPPP required under WV/NPDES Construction Stormwater General Permit (WVR104000) for ≥1 acre land disturbance
WV.wetlandSetbackNote: Army Corps Section 404; WVDEP Section 401 certification; stream buffer requirements under state law

WI.erosionControlStandard: Wisconsin Construction Site Best Management Practices Handbook
WI.notes: Wisconsin regulates wetlands through a state permit program coordinated with USACE. Shoreland-Wetland Zoning Ordinances required by all counties.
WI.swpppNote: SWPPP required under Wisconsin WPDES Construction General Permit (WI-S067831-3) for ≥1 acre land disturbance
WI.wetlandSetbackNote: Chapter NR 103 (water quality standards for wetlands); Wetland individual permit required through WDNR for impacts >0.1 acre; 30-ft setback from wetlands in many shoreland ordinances

WY.erosionControlStandard: Wyoming Stormwater Program for Construction Activities
WY.swpppNote: SWPPP required under Wyoming NPDES Construction General Permit (WYR10I000) for ≥1 acre land disturbance
WY.wetlandSetbackNote: Army Corps Section 404; WDEQ Section 401 certification

AL.lienFilingDeadlineNote: 120 days (4 months) from last furnishing of labor or materials
AL.notes: Homestead exemptions may limit lien rights on owner-occupied residential property.

AK.lienFilingDeadlineNote: 120 days from completion of construction; 15-day written notice of lien rights required for subcontractors before first furnishing
AK.notes: Notice of right to lien required within 5 days of first furnishing. 10-day notice of intent to lien required before filing.
AK.preliminaryNoticeDeadline: Before or within 5 days after first furnishing

AZ.lienFilingDeadlineNote: 120 days from completion, abandonment, or last furnishing of materials
AZ.notes: Preliminary 20-day notice required for all parties except GC with direct contract with owner. Residential properties have same rules.

AR.lienFilingDeadlineNote: 120 days from last furnishing of labor or materials
AR.notes: Foreclosure suit must be filed within 15 months of last furnishing. Residential property has special notice requirements.
AR.preliminaryNoticeDeadline: Within 10 days of first furnishing for suppliers

CA.lienFilingDeadlineNote: 90 days from completion of the project (notice of completion triggers 30-day filing period for GCs, 60 days for subs/suppliers)
CA.notes: Preliminary 20-day notice required for all claimants except direct contractors. If owner records Notice of Completion: GC has 60 days, subs/suppliers have 30 days. Joint check protections available. Most robust lien law in the US.

CO.lienFilingDeadlineNote: 120 days from last date of furnishing labor or materials
CO.notes: No preliminary notice required. Lien statement must be filed within 4 months. Must commence foreclosure within 6 months of last furnishing or date lien filed.

CT.lienFilingDeadlineNote: 90 days from last day of furnishing services or materials
CT.notes: Certificate of lien must be filed within 90 days. No preliminary notice required. Must serve copy of certificate on property owner within 30 days of filing.

DE.citation: Del. Code Ann. tit. 25, § 2701 et seq.
DE.lienFilingDeadlineNote: Within 120 days of last work: 60 days for original contractors; 90 days for subcontractors and suppliers from notice of completion
DE.notes: Suit must commence within 1 year of lien filing. Statement of lien filed with Prothonotary.

DC.citation: District of Columbia mechanics lien statutes and Superior Court filing procedures
DC.lienFilingDeadlineNote: Advisory baseline: file promptly after last furnishing; verify claimant-specific statutory timing before filing
DC.notes: Public property is generally not lienable; verify notice and filing prerequisites against current District statute before action.

FL.lienFilingDeadlineNote: 90 days from last furnishing of labor or materials on the project
FL.notes: Notice to Owner (NTO) required for all lienors except direct contractors. NTO must be served before first furnishing or within 45 days. Claim of lien must be filed within 90 days of last furnishing. Foreclose within 1 year of recording.
FL.preliminaryNoticeDeadline: Before first furnishing or within 45 days of first furnishing

GA.lienFilingDeadlineNote: Within 90 days of completion of the contract or last work performed
GA.notes: Preliminary notice (Notice to Contractor) required for subs within 30 days of first furnishing. Claim of lien must be filed within 90 days of completion. Must commence foreclosure within 365 days of lien filing.

HI.lienFilingDeadlineNote: 45 days from completion or abandonment of the improvement
HI.notes: Notice of lien rights required within 10 days for subcontractors. Claim of lien filed within 45 days. Must commence suit within 1 year of last furnishing.
HI.preliminaryNoticeDeadline: Before or within 10 days of first furnishing

ID.lienFilingDeadlineNote: 90 days from completion or cessation of labor
ID.notes: Claim of lien filed within 90 days of completion. Suit must be commenced within 6 months of filing. Residential property: notice to owner required.
ID.preliminaryNoticeDeadline: Within 5 days of first furnishing for some claimants

IL.citation: 770 ILCS 60/1 et seq. (Illinois Mechanics Lien Act)
IL.lienFilingDeadlineNote: 4 months from last date of furnishing labor or materials
IL.notes: Subcontractors must send 90-day notice to owner to preserve lien rights. Two-year foreclosure period. Owner-occupied residential under $10,000 exempt.

IN.lienFilingDeadlineNote: 90 days from completion of work or last furnishing
IN.notes: Prior notice not required to file lien but sub-contractors must notify owner within 60 days of first furnishing to preserve rights against owner. Lien statement filed within 90 days of last work.
IN.preliminaryNoticeDeadline: Within 60 days of first furnishing for subs/suppliers to give notice to owner

IA.lienFilingDeadlineNote: 90 days from last date of furnishing labor or materials
IA.notes: Subcontractors must serve preliminary notice on owner within 30 days of first furnishing. Lien statement filed within 90 days. Foreclosure within 2 years.

KS.lienFilingDeadlineNote: 4 months from completion of contract or last furnishing
KS.notes: Material suppliers must give notice of furnishing within 3 months. Lien statement filed within 4 months of completion. Foreclosure within 1 year of lien filing.
KS.preliminaryNoticeDeadline: Within 3 months of first furnishing for suppliers

KY.lienFilingDeadlineNote: 6 months from last furnishing of labor or materials
KY.notes: No preliminary notice required. Statement of lien must be filed within 6 months. Foreclosure suit must be filed within 1 year of the debt becoming due.

LA.citation: La. Rev. Stat. § 9:4801 et seq. (Louisiana Private Works Act)
LA.lienFilingDeadlineNote: 60 days from filing of Notice of Termination of Work or substantial completion
LA.notes: Owner must file Notice of Termination to trigger 60-day period; without this, claimants have up to 1 year from last work. Statement of claim filed in mortgage records.
LA.preliminaryNoticeDeadline: Notice of contract must be filed within 30 days of contract execution

ME.citation: Me. Rev. Stat. tit. 10, § 3251 et seq.
ME.lienFilingDeadlineNote: 90 days from last furnishing of labor or materials
ME.notes: No preliminary notice required. Attested statement of lien must be filed within 90 days. Foreclose within 1 year of last furnishing.

MD.citation: Md. Code Ann., Real Prop. § 9-101 et seq.
MD.lienFilingDeadlineNote: Within 180 days of last day of furnishing labor or materials
MD.notes: Preliminary notice required from subcontractors and material suppliers within 120 days of first furnishing. Petition for mechanics lien filed in circuit court within 180 days.
MD.preliminaryNoticeDeadline: Within 120 days of first furnishing for subcontractors

MA.lienFilingDeadlineNote: 90 days from last date of furnishing labor or materials
MA.notes: Subcontractors must give notice to owner within 30 days of first furnishing to preserve lien rights. Statement of lien recorded within 90 days. Commence enforcement within 90 days of recording.
MA.preliminaryNoticeDeadline: Within 30 days of first furnishing for subcontractors and suppliers

MI.lienFilingDeadlineNote: 90 days from last day of furnishing labor or materials
MI.notes: Notice of furnishing required within 20 days for subcontractors and suppliers to preserve lien rights. Claim of lien recorded within 90 days. Residential lien fund available for homeowners.

MN.lienFilingDeadlineNote: 120 days from last furnishing of labor or materials
MN.notes: Pre-lien notice required within 45 days of first furnishing for subcontractors. Lien statement filed within 120 days. Foreclosure commenced within 1 year.

MS.lienFilingDeadlineNote: 1 year from last furnishing of labor or materials
MS.notes: Mississippi has a relatively long 1-year filing period. No preliminary notice required. Enforcement suit within 1 year of lien filing. Residential homestead exemptions apply.

MO.lienFilingDeadlineNote: 6 months from last furnishing of labor or materials
MO.notes: Material suppliers must give written notice to owner within 10 days of first delivery. Just and true account filed within 6 months. Suit commenced within 1 year.
MO.preliminaryNoticeDeadline: Within 10 days of first delivery for suppliers to owners

MT.lienFilingDeadlineNote: 90 days from last furnishing of labor or materials
MT.notes: Notice of right to lien must be served on owner within 20 days of first furnishing. Claim filed within 90 days. Suit commenced within 2 years.

NE.lienFilingDeadlineNote: 120 days from last furnishing of labor or materials
NE.notes: Material suppliers must give notice within 10 days. Lien filed within 120 days. Foreclosure within 2 years.
NE.preliminaryNoticeDeadline: Within 10 days of first furnishing for suppliers

NV.lienFilingDeadlineNote: 90 days from last furnishing or from date work stopped
NV.notes: Preliminary notice (Notice of Right to Lien) required within 31 days of first furnishing for subs/suppliers. Notice of lien must be filed within 90 days. Suit within 6 months.

NH.lienFilingDeadlineNote: 120 days (4 months) from last furnishing of labor or materials
NH.notes: No preliminary notice required. Claim must be filed within 120 days. Suit within 1 year.

NJ.citation: N.J. Stat. Ann. § 2A:44A-1 et seq. (New Jersey Construction Lien Law)
NJ.lienFilingDeadlineNote: 90 days from last date of providing labor or materials
NJ.notes: No preliminary notice required. Lien claim filed within 90 days of last furnishing. Copy served on owner within 30 days of filing. Suit within 1 year of filing.

NM.lienFilingDeadlineNote: 120 days from the date that substantial completion occurs or work is abandoned
NM.notes: Notice of right to lien required from subcontractors within 60 days. Claim of lien filed within 120 days. Suit within 2 years.

NY.lienFilingDeadlineNote: 8 months from last furnishing on private projects; 30 days on public projects from final payment date
NY.notes: Notice of lien filed with county clerk within 8 months on private projects. Must be verified. Foreclose or extend within 1 year of filing. Public works use Mechanic's Lien Law Art. 2.

NC.lienFilingDeadlineNote: 120 days from last furnishing of labor or materials
NC.notes: Notice to lien agent required for projects over $30,000. Subs must serve notice within 15 days of first furnishing. Claim of lien on real property filed within 120 days. Commence action within 180 days of filing.
NC.preliminaryNoticeDeadline: Within 15 days of first furnishing for subs/suppliers

ND.lienFilingDeadlineNote: 90 days from last furnishing of labor or materials
ND.notes: No preliminary notice required. Verified statement of lien filed within 90 days. Suit within 3 years of filing.

OH.lienFilingDeadlineNote: 75 days from last furnishing of labor or materials; 60 days from original contractor's last work
OH.notes: Subcontractors must serve Notice of Furnishing within 21 days of first furnishing. Affidavit of mechanics lien filed within 75 days. Suit within 6 years of filing.
OH.preliminaryNoticeDeadline: Within 21 days of first furnishing for subcontractors

OK.citation: Okla. Stat. Ann. tit. 42, § 141 et seq.
OK.lienFilingDeadlineNote: 90 days from completion of work or last furnishing
OK.notes: Written notice to owner by subs required within 75 days of first furnishing. Claim filed within 90 days of last work. Suit within 1 year.

OR.lienFilingDeadlineNote: 75 days from completion of construction for subcontractors; 120 days for original contractors
OR.notes: Notice of right to lien must be served on owner within 8 days of first furnishing for subs/suppliers. Claim of lien filed within 75 days (subs) or 120 days (GC). Enforcement within 120 days of filing.

PA.citation: 49 Pa. Stat. Ann. § 1101 et seq. (Pennsylvania Mechanics' Lien Law)
PA.lienFilingDeadlineNote: 6 months from last furnishing of labor or materials
PA.notes: Subcontractors must file a preliminary notice with the prothonotary within 30 days of first furnishing. Claim of lien filed within 6 months. Suit within 2 years.
PA.preliminaryNoticeDeadline: Within 30 days of first furnishing for subs

RI.lienFilingDeadlineNote: 200 days from last furnishing of labor or materials; if completion posted, 100 days from posting
RI.notes: No preliminary notice required. Notice of intention to claim lien filed within 200 days. Suit within 1 year of filing.

SC.lienFilingDeadlineNote: 90 days from last furnishing of labor or materials
SC.notes: Subcontractors must serve notice within 30 days of first furnishing. Mechanic's lien filed within 90 days. Enforcement within 1 year.
SC.preliminaryNoticeDeadline: Within 30 days of first furnishing for subs

SD.lienFilingDeadlineNote: 120 days from last furnishing of labor or materials
SD.notes: No preliminary notice required. Statement of lien filed within 120 days. Suit within 6 years.

TN.lienFilingDeadlineNote: 90 days from last furnishing of labor or materials
TN.notes: Notice of nonpayment required from remote contractors. Lien notice filed within 90 days. 10-day notice of intent to lien required. Suit within 1 year.
TN.preliminaryNoticeDeadline: Within 90 days of first furnishing (remote contractors notice to prime)

TX.lienFilingDeadlineNote: Affidavit filed by 15th day of 4th calendar month after day work completed (residential: 3rd month); constitutional lien attaches automatically
TX.notes: Texas has dual lien system: constitutional (auto-attaches) and statutory. Statutory lien requires monthly notices by 15th of 2nd month following furnishing for each unpaid month. Residential projects have different rules under Ch. 53. Deadline calculated from last work month. Highly technical - errors forfeit rights.
TX.preliminaryNoticeDeadline: Monthly notice by 15th of 2nd month following furnishing

UT.lienFilingDeadlineNote: 90 days from completion of the original contract or last furnishing
UT.notes: Preliminary notice required within 20 days of first furnishing for all lienors except direct contractors. Lien filed within 90 days of completion. Suit within 12 months.

VT.lienFilingDeadlineNote: 180 days from last furnishing of labor or materials
VT.notes: No preliminary notice required. Statement of lien recorded within 180 days. Suit within 1 year.

VA.lienFilingDeadlineNote: 90 days from last furnishing of labor or materials
VA.notes: Subcontractors must give notice to owner of intent to file lien within 150 days of last furnishing. Memorandum of mechanic's lien filed within 90 days. Action to enforce within 6 months.

WA.lienFilingDeadlineNote: 90 days from last furnishing of labor or materials
WA.notes: Notice to owner/prime required within 60 days of first furnishing for subs/suppliers. Claim of lien filed within 90 days. Suit within 8 months.

WV.lienFilingDeadlineNote: 100 days from last furnishing of labor or materials
WV.notes: No preliminary notice required. Verified notice of lien filed within 100 days. Suit within 1 year.

WI.lienFilingDeadlineNote: 6 months from last furnishing of labor or materials
WI.notes: Subcontractors and suppliers must serve owner/prime with notice within 60 days of first furnishing. Claim for lien filed within 6 months. Suit within 2 years.
WI.preliminaryNoticeDeadline: Within 60 days of first furnishing for subs/suppliers

WY.lienFilingDeadlineNote: 150 days from last furnishing of labor or materials
WY.notes: No preliminary notice required. Lien filed within 150 days. Suit within 180 days of lien filing.

ND.lawScope: No general prevailing wage law; federal Davis-Bacon applies to federally funded projects

VA.administeredBy: Virginia Department of Labor and Industry
VA.notes: Virginia enacted a prevailing wage law effective May 1, 2021 (Code of Va. § 2.2-4321.3).

WA.administeredBy: Washington Department of Labor and Industries (L&I)

WV.lawScope: No general prevailing wage law; federal Davis-Bacon applies to federally funded projects
WV.notes: West Virginia repealed its prevailing wage law in 2016.

WI.lawScope: No general prevailing wage law; federal Davis-Bacon applies to federally funded projects
WI.notes: Wisconsin repealed its prevailing wage law in 2017-2018.

WY.lawScope: No general prevailing wage law; federal Davis-Bacon applies to federally funded projects

AL.interestRateNote: 1% per month on late payments on public contracts
AL.notes: Alabama Prompt Payment Act covers public works. Private projects governed by contract terms.
AL.retainageReleaseNote: Released within 60 days of final completion and acceptance

AK.interestRateNote: Interest at 1.5% per month (18% annually) on overdue amounts
AK.notes: Alaska has both public and limited private prompt payment protections. Pay-if-paid clauses are recognized but subject to equitable limits.
AK.retainageReleaseNote: Retainage reduced to 5% after 50% completion; released within 30 days of final acceptance

AZ.interestRateNote: Interest at 1% per month on outstanding balance after due date
AZ.notes: Prompt payment applies to both public and private construction. 7 days after owner payment to pass through.
AZ.retainageReleaseNote: Retainage released within 60 days of substantial completion

AR.interestRateNote: Interest at 1.5% per month (18% annually) on overdue amounts
AR.notes: Covers state and local government contracts. Private sector governed by contract terms.
AR.retainageReleaseNote: Released within 30 days of final completion

CA.citation: Cal. Pub. Cont. Code § 7107; Cal. Civ. Code § 8800 et seq.
CA.interestRateNote: Interest at 2% per month on late public contract payments; private at legal rate
CA.notes: California does not enforce pay-if-paid clauses on construction contracts. Both public and private projects covered. Strong protections for subcontractors and suppliers.
CA.retainageReleaseNote: Public works retainage reduced to 5%; released within 60 days of final completion

CO.interestRateNote: Interest at 1% per month (12% annually) on late amounts
CO.notes: Public Works Prompt Payment Act covers state and local public works. 5% retainage cap after 50% completion on public projects.
CO.retainageReleaseNote: Retainage released within 60 days of substantial completion on public works

CT.interestRateNote: Interest at 1% per month on overdue payments
CT.notes: Prompt Payment Act covers public construction contracts. Retainage reduced to 5% on public works.
CT.retainageReleaseNote: Retainage released within 45 days of final acceptance on public contracts

DE.interestRateNote: Interest at 1% per month on late amounts
DE.notes: Public works prompt payment statute. Private projects governed by contract.
DE.retainageReleaseNote: Released within 30 days of final acceptance

DC.citation: District construction payment and procurement requirements (public contracts) and contract law principles
DC.interestRateNote: Advisory baseline of 1% per month for overdue balances unless contract/governing rule differs
DC.notes: Use as advisory scheduling guidance. Confirm project-specific contract language and District procurement clauses before enforcement decisions.
DC.retainageReleaseNote: Retainage release timing is contract and procurement-rule dependent; track closeout milestones

FL.citation: Fla. Stat. § 255.073 et seq. (public); Fla. Stat. § 715.12 (private)
FL.interestRateNote: Interest at 1% per month on overdue amounts (or current prevailing interest rate, whichever is greater)
FL.notes: Florida has one of the most comprehensive prompt payment laws. Covers both public and private construction. Owner must pay within 25 days of invoice; GC to sub within 10 days of receipt of payment.
FL.retainageReleaseNote: Reduced to 5% after 50% completion; released within 20 days of final payment

GA.interestRateNote: Interest at 1% per month on overdue amounts
GA.notes: Georgia Prompt Payment Act covers both public and private commercial construction. Owner has 15 days after receipt of payment request.
GA.retainageReleaseNote: Released within 60 days of final completion

HI.interestRateNote: Interest at 1.5% per month on late payments
HI.notes: Public works prompt payment law. Retainage limited to 5% after 50% completion on state contracts.
HI.retainageReleaseNote: Released within 30 days of final acceptance

ID.interestRateNote: Interest at 1% per month on overdue amounts after 30 days
ID.notes: Idaho prompt payment law covers public construction. Retainage reduced to 5% after 50% completion.
ID.retainageReleaseNote: Released within 30 days of substantial completion

IL.citation: 30 ILCS 550/1 et seq. (State Prompt Payment Act); 815 ILCS 603 (Private)
IL.interestRateNote: Interest at 2% per month on late payments under public contracts
IL.notes: Illinois has both public and private prompt payment laws. The Illinois Private Prompt Payment Act (815 ILCS 603) covers private commercial construction.
IL.retainageReleaseNote: Released within 30 days of final completion and acceptance

IN.interestRateNote: Interest at 1% per month on overdue payments
IN.notes: Public works prompt payment law. Owner has 35 days from receipt of payment request.
IN.retainageReleaseNote: Released within 60 days of final acceptance

IA.interestRateNote: Interest at 1% per month on overdue amounts
IA.notes: Iowa public works prompt payment. 5% retainage cap on public projects.
IA.retainageReleaseNote: Released within 30 days of final acceptance

KS.interestRateNote: Interest at 1% per month on late amounts
KS.notes: Public works prompt payment law. 5% retainage cap on public contracts.
KS.retainageReleaseNote: Released within 30 days of substantial completion
